message:
test(fcp-core): pin PolicyBundle hash + signing-bytes determinism (flywheel_connectors-j42c7)

Bead asks for ManifestBundle hash determinism. fcp-core has no type
literally named ManifestBundle; the closest analogues with a
documented bundle-hash contract are compute_policy_bundle_hash
(policy.rs:280) — the canonical function that computes a deterministic
blake3-256:<hex> digest over a PolicyBundle's canonicalized payload —
and PolicyBundle::signing_bytes (policy.rs:381) which downstream
signers feed Ed25519.

Coverage:
- Same inputs produce the same hash across calls (basic determinism)
- Policy permutations produce the same hash — the function sorts by
  (object_id, schema_id, object_hash) before hashing, so input order
  is irrelevant
- Distinguishing inputs produce different hashes:
  bundle_id, policy_seq, zone_id, policy reference content, both
  presence-of-Some-vs-None and value-difference for previous_bundle
  and created_at
- Empty policies rejected with InvalidBundle naming the field
- Hash output format pinned: starts with "blake3-256:" prefix,
  64-char lowercase ASCII hex suffix
- PolicyBundle::signing_bytes is deterministic across multiple calls
- PolicyBundle::signing_bytes captures the bundle_hash field (a
  changed bundle_hash produces different signing input, validating
  that the bundle hash is one of the signed fields)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
